pages.full_text:
Tuesday October 9th.

[photo of Pegasus Bridge]

Pegasus Bridge Bénouville  6th June 1944
first bridge liberated . Originally built 1932.
 Rebuilt 1994
photo_captions: Pegasus Bridge Bénouville 6th June 1944 first bridge liberated . Originally built 1932. Rebuilt 1994
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a black-and-white photograph of Pegasus Bridge. Caption text is handwritten below the photo. Date 'Tuesday October 9th.' appears above the photo as a heading. No year is stated on this page; context from right-hand page (Oct 9th entry) and mention of the museum being 'only built a couple of years ago' alongside 'Rebuilt 1994' suggests a date in the mid-to-late 1990s, but the year cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
During the night there were [illegible/cut] light houses & lighted marker buoys. lovely sound of the sea.

 Oct 9th Tuesday.
  As we left, the van got stuck on [cut] the wet grass & mud. Then we scraped [cut] one of the water brackets on the drive.
  We drove through __Deauville__ - beautifu[l cut] large old houses. We stopped at Villervill[e cut] for coffee. I walked down to the sea [cut] across the sandy beach to collect shells. [cut] Simon fixed the water tank. Travelling wes[t cut] we reached __Pegasus Bridge__ & looked at the museum there before and after lunch The museum was only built a couple of years ago. It was the start of the Allied offensive back into Europe on D Day. The bridge, & the neighbouring bridge across the R. Orne, had to be taken from the [?Grem] Germans intact.
  [struck-through word] Bréville was badly damaged, on the ridge.
pages.notes: Text is cut off at the right margin throughout the page — this is a scan edge issue, not damaged text. Several line-ends are missing a word or two. One word before 'Germans intact' appears struck through (possibly 'Grem' or a false start). A word at the start of the last sentence is also struck through before 'Bréville'. No year stated; volume context (1989–2003) and reference to the Pegasus Bridge museum being 'only built a couple of years ago' (museum opened c.1992) suggests mid-1990s, possibly 1996 or later — cannot confirm year from page alone. Page number in top-right corner appears partially visible; could not confidently read a full number.
